gateway_offline_message: The Home Gateway routing this page is not responding right now.
peer_gateway_hint: Enable if this peer is a Home Gateway in your local network
gateway_download_warning: WARNING: tokens will be regenerated — current gateway will be invalidated
gateway_download_confirm: Downloading regenerates the gateway tokens. The currently running gateway will lose its connection. Continue?
gateway_tokens_warning_body: These tokens are shown only now. Copy them to your password manager or download the .env file. After closing, they cannot be retrieved without re-pairing.
gateway_tokens_env_hint: This content goes to /config/gateway.env on your gateway host.
gateway_deploy_lxc_intro: Run the command below on your Proxmox host shell. The installer will create a Debian 12 LXC, redeem the pairing token automatically, and start the gateway service.
gateway_deploy_lxc_command_hint: Pairing token is valid for 10 minutes and can only be used once.
gateway_delete_warning_body: Removing this gateway peer permanently deletes its WireGuard credentials and revokes its server access. Any existing tunnel will drop and the agent will start failing on its next heartbeat.
gateway_delete_routes_hint: These {count} routes route through this gateway. They will be set to disabled (not deleted) — you can re-enable them later by re-pointing them at a different gateway.
gateway_delete_no_routes: No routes target this gateway. Safe to delete.
gateway_delete_confirm_ip_label: Type this gateway's IP to confirm:
gateway_deploy_docker_intro: Drop the docker-compose.yml and gateway.env into a folder on your Synology / Linux host, then run 'docker compose up -d' (or use Synology Container Manager → Project → Create).
gateway_deploy_docker_intro_synology: Pick the Synology/NAS profile below, download both files, and follow the DSM 7.2+ click-through steps. Container Manager will pull the image and start the gateway.
gateway_deploy_docker_intro_linux: Pick the Linux profile below, download both files into a folder on your host (e.g. /opt/gatecontrol-gateway/), and run 'docker compose up -d'. systemd starts the container on boot via Docker's restart policy.
gateway_deploy_docker_platform_synology: Synology DSM / NAS / Docker Desktop (userspace WireGuard)
gateway_deploy_docker_platform_hint_syno: Synology DSM doesn't allow custom kernel modules, so wg-quick falls back to the wireguard-go userspace daemon. We bind-mount /dev/net/tun for it. Same applies to BSD-based NAS systems and Docker Desktop on macOS/Windows. ~2-3x slower than kernel WG but in practice unnoticeable for typical home use.
gateway_deploy_docker_platform_hint_linu: Standard Linux distributions ship the WireGuard kernel module since kernel 5.6 (Debian 11+, Ubuntu 22.04+, Fedora, etc.). Kernel WG uses netlink directly — no TUN device, no userspace daemon, full link-rate performance.
gateway_deploy_docker_net_hint_host: Host mode supports L4 routes (raw TCP) and Wake-on-LAN. Required for the full feature set. Watch out for port conflicts on the NAS host (8080, 9876, plus any L4 listen ports).
gateway_deploy_docker_net_hint_bridge: Bridge mode isolates the container but disables L4-route dynamic ports and breaks Wake-on-LAN multicast. Pick this only if your host blocks network_mode: host.
gateway_deploy_docker_synology_step1: Open File Station → /docker (create the folder if it doesn't exist) → create a subfolder named gatecontrol-gateway, then inside it another subfolder named config.
gateway_deploy_docker_synology_step2: Inside config/ create a text file named gateway.env and paste the gateway.env content from above.
gateway_deploy_docker_synology_step4: Project name: gatecontrol-gateway. Path: /docker/gatecontrol-gateway. Source: 'Create docker-compose.yml' — paste the docker-compose.yml content from above.
gateway_deploy_docker_synology_step5: Click Next, accept defaults, then Build. DSM pulls the image and starts the container.
gateway_deploy_docker_synology_step6: The gateway should appear as 'online' in this dashboard within ~30 seconds.
gateway_deploy_docker_linux_step1: SSH to your Linux host as root or a user in the docker group.
gateway_deploy_docker_linux_step3: Save the docker-compose.yml from above as /opt/gatecontrol-gateway/docker-compose.yml.
gateway_deploy_docker_linux_step4: Save gateway.env as /opt/gatecontrol-gateway/config/gateway.env, then chmod 600 on it (it contains the WireGuard private key).
gateway_deploy_docker_linux_step5: Start the container:  cd /opt/gatecontrol-gateway && docker compose up -d   — Docker pulls the image and starts the service.
gateway_deploy_docker_linux_step6: Watch the logs with 'docker compose logs -f' until you see 'Gateway running'. The peer should appear as 'online' in this dashboard within ~30 seconds.

gateway_failover_settings.down_threshold: Lower values detect failures faster but react to network blips. 90 seconds is a sensible default.
